subject: Public Health
title: The Role of the WHO
description: Learn The Role of the WHO in one minute.

hook.text: Founded in 1948, the World Health Organization coordinates global health for 194 member states.

buildup.text: It sets international health standards, tracks diseases, and leads emergency responses.

discovery.text: The WHO led the eradication of smallpox in 1980—the only human disease ever eliminated.

twist.text: The WHO has no enforcement power—it relies entirely on member states to follow its guidance.

climax.text: During COVID-19, the WHO's authority and funding structure faced intense scrutiny and debate.

punchline.text: Global health needs a coordinator. The WHO is all we have.

quiz.question: What major achievement did the WHO lead?
